metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3999
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 4 B and C: NECAP1 knockdown displays a reduction in AP2 puncta at the plasma membrane and also an increased intensity in the ones that were stained. The bottom GFP images are to display the transduction efficiency of the virus. Figure 4C quantifies AP2 puncta in NECAP1 KD and WT condition.
Figure 4 D: The colocalisation of AP-2 with Clathrin makes it a reliable indicator of a functional vesicle formation site.
Figure 4 F-H: Quantifications from 3D super-resolution microscopy demonstrate that the diameter of the vesicle formation site increases in all three dimensions in cases of NECAP1 KD.
Figure 4J, S3: In order to verify if NECAP1 KD increases the size of the Clathrin coated pits or coated plaques, the dimensions of AP-2-labeled structures on the membrane contacting the coverslip (bottom) and on the cell surface facing the culture medium were compared. Both locations show the same 3D increase in diameter.
Figure 4 K: Electron microscopy displays the increase in size but not in the number of clusters of clathrin-coated pits.
Figure S4 C: Quantification of EM of NECAP1 KD and control in hippocampal primary neurons show a similar shift in the increase of population of synaptic vesicles with greater SV diameter.
Figure S4 D: Bar graph displays the mean SV diameter of NECAP1 KD neurons is significantly higher than the control.

Note: Figures 4B-K are from COS7 cells, while Figure S4C and S4D are derived from hippocampal neurons.. Experimental description: Most of the experiments were conducted in Cos7 cells, however, hippocampal neurons were extracted from the rat. The species information was not provided.
